description: Canonical workload registry. Single source of truth mapping legacy workload IDs (used in immutable results CSVs and provenance filters) to descriptive canonical IDs and paper-facing display names, with per-workload metadata. Consumers (figure scripts, table generators, OpenWhisk config/schedule loader) MUST resolve display names through config/workload_registry.py rather than hard-coding the mapping. Results CSV 'workload' columns and verifier source_filter values keep the legacy IDs and are NOT rewritten; the registry normalizes for presentation only.
notes: Legacy IDs A/B/C/C_mixed/C_hit/Z were adopted before the real-YCSB migration and are easily confused with the standard YCSB core workloads A-F. Descriptive names remove that ambiguity. CHURN is a database-evolution mutation schedule, not a measured evaluation workload.

workloads.request_distribution: scrambled Zipf(alpha=0.99) over a 100000-key space; rank->random permutation so hot keys are scattered across the key range
workloads.active_keys: ~23k unique of 100000 key space; top-1 key ~7.8% of ops
workloads.hit_semantics: pure hit (all lookups in-range; zero not-found)
workloads.paper_role: controlled read workload; broad-working-set with genuine Zipfian skew (skewed-hotset case).

workloads.hit_semantics: pure hit (all lookups in-range; zero not-found)
workloads.paper_role: controlled read workload; broad-working-set with no hotspot (uniform case).

workloads.request_distribution: uniform over [590000,609999], 5 copies each (20000 unique keys, each exactly 5x)
workloads.hit_semantics: MIXED: ~50% not-found (keys 600001..609999 are out-of-range negative lookups). Every not-found probe descends the B+tree right edge to the rightmost real leaf, concentrating traffic on a key-range-induced right-boundary hotspot.
workloads.paper_role: controlled read workload; narrow small-working-set tail-boundary case. Result is bimodal across seeds and driven by the ~50% not-found right-boundary probe concentration, not general tail prediction.
workloads.must_annotate: Always mark ~50% not-found and right-boundary (rightmost-leaf) probe concentration.

workloads.request_distribution: uniform over [580001,600000], 5 copies each (20000 unique keys, each exactly 5x)
workloads.hit_semantics: pure hit (all keys exist; zero not-found by construction, hard-asserted in the generator manifest)
workloads.paper_role: pure-hit control for Tail-Mixed: same 20k tail-region key-space size and locality but with the not-found effect removed, isolating whether frequency-aware prefetch still helps once the right-edge not-found artifact is gone.
workloads.must_annotate: Always mark as the control that removes the not-found effect.

workloads.request_distribution: Zipf(alpha=0.99) over 1000 keys, NOT scrambled (rank == key), so the hot keys are low-id and clustered
workloads.hit_semantics: pure hit (all lookups in-range; zero not-found)
workloads.paper_role: hotspot-location control (low-key concentrated hotspot) for the N-sweep; used to show the plateau shape is consistent across hotspot placement.

workloads.generator: workloads/gen_workload.py (type=YD); deterministic Python reconstruction of YCSB-D (read-latest) semantics
workloads.request_distribution: read-latest: reads draw Zipf(alpha=0.99) rank over the CURRENT item count with rank 0 mapped to the newest key, so the read hotspot rides the tail of a growing keyspace (non-stationary / moving hotspot). op[0] forced to a read.
workloads.active_keys: dataset [1..600000]; inserts append 600001.. and grow the current max
workloads.hit_semantics: reads target the current keyspace (in-range hits); insert stream ages the DB via the aging path
workloads.paper_role: deterministic Python reconstruction of YCSB-D semantics; moving-hotspot static-plan staleness test (non-stationary). NOT an official YCSB-D trace.

workloads.generator: workloads/gen_workload.py (type=YE); deterministic Python reconstruction of YCSB-E (short-ranges) semantics
workloads.request_distribution: short-ranges: scan starts are scrambled Zipf(alpha=0.99) over [1..600000] (scattered hot starts, like Scattered-Zipf); scan length uniform [1,100] (stationary hotspot). op[0] forced to a read.
workloads.hit_semantics: scan starts in-range; insert stream ages the DB via the aging path
workloads.paper_role: deterministic Python reconstruction of YCSB-E semantics; stationary-hotspot control against Latest-Aging in the static-plan staleness test. NOT an official YCSB-E trace.

workloads.op_mix: repeating 10-op block [rmw, insert, update, update, read, rmw, insert, update, scan, read]; the harness remaps readmodifywrite to DELETE
workloads.request_distribution: inserts sequential from 600001; rmw/update/read/scan keys sampled uniform-without-replacement over [1,600000]; scan count 128
workloads.active_keys: n/a (mutation stream, not a read hotset)
workloads.paper_role: database-evolution / mutation schedule that ages the DB between measured read probes. NOT a measured evaluation workload and NOT latency-measured.
